finding: single-tool wrapper showed the tool name+server in BOTH the outer group header AND the inner McpToolCallUI/McpToolUseRenderer card — a visual regression vs the old bare card (name once)
the 'agreement' unit test was tautological — it re-derived `consumed` from the same shouldWrapRun call instead of exercising the real contentSpan/render branch, so it could not catch a future desync

no desync possible: contentSpan and the McpToolUseGroup render branch both call shouldWrapRun(collectToolRun(blocks,index)) on the identical props within one render, so wrap decision and consumed count can never diverge
single-artifact wrap rendered the tool name twice (group header + inner card) — cosmetic duplication, not a logic error

hasArtifactInRun matches any tool_result with resource_links without checking ownership, so an orphan tool_result(tool_use_id=B) in a run with a different lone tool_use could wrap/mislabel — unreachable in the normal flow (resolveArtifactToolUseId + normalizeToolResultOrder only assign present ids)

header counts tool_use by content_type while the wrap gate counts by runToolUseIds (id-bearing), so a degenerate id-less tool_use could make the header count differ from cards rendered — tool_use blocks always carry an id in practice
module-level scrolledApprovals Set is never cleared → bounded-by-approval-count session growth (tiny leak)

after an approve/deny POST failure the tool reverts to pending_approval + remounts, but the id is already in the Set → the re-surfaced prompt is NOT re-scrolled; acceptable (the user just interacted with it)

no concurrency hazard: bubbleBlocks is one const snapshot per render, both contentSpan + component read it synchronously, streaming re-renders recompute from pure fns, and the Set mutates only in a commit-phase effect (StrictMode-safe)

a live single artifact tool renders first as a plain card then re-wraps into the auto-opened group when the tool_result arrives (a mid-stream reflow) — functionally correct, contentSpan + render stay in lockstep

removing the group-toggle click relies on the artifact auto-open latch; both callers always seed resource_links so it auto-opens, but a future empty-links caller would get a collapsed group (a false-FAIL the suite still guards, not a false-green)

scrollIntoView honors prefers-reduced-motion but does not move focus or emit an aria-live announcement, so AT/keyboard users are not told the approval appeared (a broader a11y improvement beyond this scroll fix; pre-existing approval card has no live region)
single-tool wrapper header truncates the tool name with no title/aria-label (mirrors pre-existing McpToolUseRenderer header — no new divergence)

destructuring Stores.McpServer.servers subscribes EVERY McpToolGroupCard (incl. multi-tool where servers is unused) → re-render of all group cards on any server-list change; matches the McpToolUseRenderer pattern, negligible (server list rarely changes)
the scrolledApprovals Set grows unbounded over the session (one id per approval) — a slow, bounded-by-approval-count leak

singleUse.name/.server_id (MessageContentDataToolUse), server.display_name (McpServer), hasArtifactInRun resource_links (MessageContentDataToolResult) all match api-client types; shouldWrapRun reads MessageContent shapes correctly
shouldWrapRun follows the pure-helper idiom in toolRun.ts (reuses runToolUseIds/hasArtifactInRun) and the single-tool header mirrors McpToolUseRenderer's server-label resolution — conformant
the scroll effect's eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps with a mount-only [] dep array matches ~32 existing precedents — conformant

the scrollIntoView spy genuinely verifies the feature (asserts the specific tool-approval-<id> element scrolled with behavior:'smooth'); the hardcoded 'smooth' is only brittle to a future playwright reducedMotion:'reduce' config
shouldWrapRun / singleUse / singleServerLabel / scrolledApprovals are clear and consistent with existing names — no naming issue
toolUses[0].content cast to MessageContentDataToolUse was unguarded (matches the sibling unguarded cast at :309), so an undefined-content tool_use would throw
window.matchMedia was called after a typeof window guard but not existence-checked — a window-without-matchMedia host would throw (browser-only component, low risk)
